revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 甲山寺 |画像 = Kōyama-ji, Main Hall 001.jpg |画像サイズ=250 |画像説明 = 本堂 |所在地 = [[香川県]][[善通寺市]]弘田町1765番地1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|13|59.5|N|133|45|56.75|E|type:landmark_region:JP-37|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 医王山 |院号 = 多宝院 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗善通寺派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = （伝）[[弘仁]]12年（[[821年]]） |開山 = |開基 = （伝）[[空海]]（弘法大師） |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 医王山 多宝院 甲山寺 |別称 = |札所等 = [[四国八十八箇所]]74番 |文化財 = |公式HP = https://koyamaji.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = [香川] 医王山 多宝院 甲山寺 |地図2={{Switcher | {{Location map+|Japan Kagawa|width=230|caption= |float=center|places= {{Location map~|Japan Kagawa|lat_deg=34|lat_min=13|lat_sec=59.5|lon_deg=133|lon_min=45|lon_sec=56.75|mark=Indian_Swastika.svg|marksize=12|position=left|label=甲山寺|label_size=100|caption=}} {{Location map~|Japan Kagawa|lat_deg=34|lat_min=21|lat_sec=2.46|lon_deg=134|lon_min=2|lon_sec=48.98|mark=blue pog.svg|position=top|label=[[高松駅 (香川県)|高松駅]]|label_size=95}} {{Location map~|Japan Kagawa|lat_deg=34|lat_min=12|lat_sec=51|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=56|mark=BSicon FLUGg.svg|marksize=10|position=bottom|label=[[高松空港]]|label_size=95}}}} | 香川県における位置 | {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=230}} | 善通寺市内での位置 }} }} '''甲山寺'''（こうやまじ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]にある[[真言宗善通寺派]]の[[寺院]]。医王山、院号は多宝院と号す。[[本尊]]は[[薬師如来]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]第七十四番[[札所]]。[[甲山 (香川県)|甲山]]（かぶとやま）の麓に位置する。 * 本尊[[真言]]：おん ころころ せんだりまとうぎ そわか * [[ご詠歌]]：十二神（じゅうにしん）味方に持てる戦（いくさ）には おのれとこころ甲（かぶと）やまかな * 納経印：当寺本尊、多聞天堂毘沙門天、うさぎの朱印（200円）
== 概要 == [[File:Kouyamaji20210309 1.jpg|170px|left|thumb|甲山]] 伝説では、壮年期の空海が寺を建立しようと幼きとき遊んだこのあたりを探索していると老翁が現れ、この地にお寺を建立すべしとお告げをした。この地の岩窟に住む毘沙門天の化身と悟った空海はこの岩窟に毘沙門天を祀った。 その後、嵯峨天皇の勅命を受け別当として[[満濃池]]の修築を命じられた空海は、この地で薬師如来を刻み修法すると数万の人々が集まり、無事に築造を完成に導いた。[[弘仁]]12年（[[821年]]）その功績に対して朝廷から二万銭が与えられ、その一部によって堂を建立したのが当寺の始まりであるという。
天正年間の兵火により本尊とわずかの寺宝が残ったのみで、『四国徧礼霊場記』（1689年刊）には、「むかし大伽藍の所といえども荒涼せり盛衰は世の数ある・・」と記されている状況であったが、享保20年（1730）に本堂、寛保2年（1742）に大師堂が再建され、『四国遍礼名所図会』（1800年刊）の挿絵では現在の形に近い形に復興している。
甲山（標高87.2 m)には、室町時代に天霧城の出城があったと云われていて、山頂に石舞台跡があり、頂上には神武天皇孝明天皇震儀石がある。また、当寺から山頂に向かう山道には江戸時代末期に開かれたという西国三十三所の石仏が並んでいる<ref>現地案内看板より</ref>。
== 伽藍 == [[File:Kouyamaji kyuusannmonn1.jpg|100px|thumb|旧山門]] [[File:Kouyamaji bisyamongutu1.jpg|100px|thumb|旧毘沙門窟]] * [[山門]]：2008年落慶 * [[中門]]：明和6年（1769）に完成の旧山門を中門として修築。 * [[本堂]]：享保20年（1735）完成。本尊を拝観できる(脱履物）。本尊は薬師如来坐像（肥松の一木造り、彫眼）、脇仏は日光月光菩薩立像、右脇陣に毘沙門天立像、左脇陣に阿弥陀如来坐像。 * [[大師堂]]：寛保2年（1742）完成。黒衣の大師像で、2014年と2020年に開帳された。 * 客殿：書道家の岡本光平氏が襖に描いた般若心経の襖書 * 護摩堂：天明2年（1782）完成。不動明王坐像と二童子。 * 多聞天堂：奥は毘沙門窟で、石を彫った[[毘沙門天]]、両脇に吉祥天と禅膩師童子が祀られている。 * 子安地蔵：大師堂に向って右前にいて、前掛けを持ち帰ると子供が生まれたというエピソードから。 * 淡島大明神（祠） * 鐘楼堂：宝暦2年（1752）完成。 * ウサギが16羽：本尊脇仏の月光菩薩の持物にウサギが描かれていることから、山門や中門の内側および茶堂の屋根と手水舎の前にいる<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |title=ようこそウサギ寺 境内に“１６匹”お守りにも 善通寺・甲山寺 |newspaper=四国新聞 |date=2020-04-03 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |accessdate=2023-07-15 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200405172931/http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |archivedate=2020-04-05 }}</ref>。 山門をくぐり右に手水舎を越え、左に曲がり中門をくぐると右手に納経所、わずかな石段を上がると本堂その右の続に護摩堂、左に数段上がると大師堂があり、鐘楼がその先にあり、その向こうに毘沙門窟入口があり、中に毘沙門天の石像が祀られている。 * [[宿坊]]：なし * 駐車場：無料大駐車場が山門前にあり * [[水神]]社（祠）：境内の一番高い所に祀られる。 * ミニ西国三十三観音めぐり：境内から山頂に向かう遊歩道に点在している。 <gallery> Kouyamaji 20201124 01.jpg|屋根瓦のウサギ Kouyamaji20210313 1.jpg|山門 Kōyama-ji, the Grounds of the Temple 001.jpg|境内 Kouyamazi 02.jpg|大師堂 Kouyamazi 03.jpg|客殿（左）と護摩堂 Kouyamazi02.jpg|多聞天堂 Kouyamaji20210309 3.jpg|中門（旧山門） Kouyamaji 20250411 1.jpg|庭園 Kōyama-ji, Auxiliary Awashima Shrine 001.jpg|淡島大明神 Kouyamaji suizin01.jpg|水神社 </gallery>
== 文化財 == ; 国の史跡 * [[遍路道#文化財|讃岐遍路道]] 甲山寺境内 - 遍路道は、甲山北側を東へと回り込み旧山門へ至り、75番善通寺へは門前の弘田川に架けられた石橋（現在は橋は無い）を渡り南へ向かった。令和6年12月20日答申。<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref> == 交通アクセス == ; 鉄道 * [[四国旅客鉄道]]（JR四国） [[土讃線]]{{Ndash}}[[善通寺駅]] (3.6 km) ; 道路 * 一般道：[[香川県道48号善通寺詫間線]] 善通寺球場 (0.3 km) * 自動車道：[[高松自動車道]] [[善通寺インターチェンジ|善通寺IC]] (4.5 km)
== 前後の札所 == ; [[四国八十八箇所]] : 73 [[出釈迦寺]] --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-- '''74 甲山寺''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[善通寺]] == 周辺 == [[File:Asanozizou 20250411 1.jpg|150px|thumb|阿瀬の地蔵菩薩堂]] * 阿瀬の地蔵菩薩 :行基作と伝えられる像高約2m、檜の寄木造りの立像。天霧山麓から戦国時代に難を避けるため当地に移された。昭和62年7月21日市有形文化財指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=宮崎建樹 |year=2007 |title=四国遍路ひとり歩き同行二人 |volume=地図編 |edition=第8版 |publisher=へんろみち保存協力会 }}
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[日本の寺院一覧]] * [[七ヶ所まいり]]
== 外部リンク == * {{official website|koyamaji.or.jp/ |医王山 多宝院 甲山寺}}（寺院公式） * {{url|https://www.88shikokuhenro.jp/74koyamaji/ |第74番札所 医王山 多宝院 甲山寺}}（四国八十八ヶ所霊場会公式） * {{url|https://7kasyomairi.sakura.ne.jp/ |七ヶ所まいり}}（七か所まいり実行委員会公式） {{四国八十八箇所}} {{Buddhism2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうやまし}} [[Category:香川県の寺]] [[Category:真言宗善通寺派の寺院]] [[Category:四国八十八箇所]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]] [[Category:善通寺市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|薬師如来|" to "|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|"

== 伽藍 == [[File:Kouyamaji kyuusannmonn1.jpg|100px|thumb|旧山門]] [[File:Kouyamaji bisyamongutu1.jpg|100px|thumb|旧毘沙門窟]] * [[山門]]：2008年落慶 * [[中門]]：明和6年（1769）に完成の旧山門を中門として修築。 * [[本堂]]：享保20年（1735）完成。本尊を拝観できる(脱履物）。本尊は薬師如来坐像（肥松の一木造り、彫眼）、脇仏は日光月光菩薩立像、右脇陣に毘沙門天立像、左脇陣に阿弥陀如来坐像。 * [[大師堂]]：寛保2年（1742）完成。黒衣の大師像で、2014年と2020年に開帳された。 * 客殿：書道家の岡本光平氏が襖に描いた般若心経の襖書 * 護摩堂：天明2年（1782）完成。不動明王坐像と二童子。 * 多聞天堂：奥は毘沙門窟で、石を彫った[[毘沙門天]]、両脇に吉祥天と禅膩師童子が祀られている。 * 子安地蔵：大師堂に向って右前にいて、前掛けを持ち帰ると子供が生まれたというエピソードから。 * 淡島大明神（祠） * 鐘楼堂：宝暦2年（1752）完成。 * ウサギが16羽：本尊脇仏の月光菩薩の持物にウサギが描かれていることから、山門や中門の内側および茶堂の屋根と手水舎の前にいる<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |title=ようこそウサギ寺 境内に“１６匹”お守りにも 善通寺・甲山寺 |newspaper=四国新聞 |date=2020-04-03 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |accessdate=2023-07-15 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200405172931/http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |archivedate=2020-04-05 }}</ref>。 山門をくぐり右に手水舎を越え、左に曲がり中門をくぐると右手に納経所、わずかな石段を上がると本堂その右の続に護摩堂、左に数段上がると大師堂があり、鐘楼がその先にあり、その向こうに毘沙門窟入口があり、中に毘沙門天の石像が祀られている。 * [[宿坊]]：なし * 駐車場：無料大駐車場が山門前にあり * [[水神]]社（祠）：境内の一番高い所に祀られる。 * ミニ西国三十三観音めぐり：境内から山頂に向かう遊歩道に点在している。 <gallery> Kouyamaji 20201124 01.jpg|屋根瓦のウサギ Kōyama-ji, the Grounds of the Temple 001.jpg|境内 Kōyama-ji, Main Hall 001.jpg|本堂 Kouyamazi 02.jpg|大師堂 Kouyamazi 03.jpg|客殿（左）と護摩堂 Kouyamazi02.jpg|多聞天堂 Kouyamaji20210309 3.jpg|中門（旧山門） Kouyamaji 20250411 1.jpg|庭園 Kōyama-ji, Auxiliary Awashima Shrine 001.jpg|淡島大明神 Kouyamaji suizin01.jpg|水神社 </gallery>
== 文化財 == ; 国の史跡 * [[遍路道#文化財|讃岐遍路道]] 甲山寺境内 - 遍路道は、甲山北側を東へと回り込み旧山門へ至り、75番善通寺へは門前の弘田川に架けられた石橋（現在は橋は無い）を渡り南へ向かった。令和6年12月20日答申。<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref> == 交通アクセス == ; 鉄道 * [[四国旅客鉄道]]（JR四国） [[土讃線]]{{Ndash}}[[善通寺駅]] (3.6 km) ; 道路 * 一般道：[[香川県道48号善通寺詫間線]] 善通寺球場 (0.3 km) * 自動車道：[[高松自動車道]] [[善通寺インターチェンジ|善通寺IC]] (4.5 km)
== 前後の札所 == ; [[四国八十八箇所]] : 73 [[出釈迦寺]] --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-- '''74 甲山寺''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[善通寺]] == 周辺 == [[File:Asanozizou 20250411 1.jpg|150px|thumb|阿瀬の地蔵菩薩堂]] * 阿瀬の地蔵菩薩 :行基作と伝えられる像高約2m、檜の寄木造りの立像。天霧山麓から戦国時代に難を避けるため当地に移された。昭和62年7月21日市有形文化財指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=宮崎建樹 |year=2007 |title=四国遍路ひとり歩き同行二人 |volume=地図編 |edition=第8版 |publisher=へんろみち保存協力会 }}
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[日本の寺院一覧]] * [[七ヶ所まいり]]
== 外部リンク == * {{official website|koyamaji.or.jp/ |医王山 多宝院 甲山寺}}（寺院公式） * {{url|https://www.88shikokuhenro.jp/74koyamaji/ |第74番札所 医王山 多宝院 甲山寺}}（四国八十八ヶ所霊場会公式） * {{url|https://7kasyomairi.sakura.ne.jp/ |七ヶ所まいり}}（七か所まいり実行委員会公式） {{四国八十八箇所}} {{Buddhism2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうやまし}} [[Category:香川県の寺]] [[Category:真言宗善通寺派の寺院]] [[Category:四国八十八箇所]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]] [[Category:善通寺市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 甲山寺 |画像 = Kouyamaji20210313 1.jpg |画像サイズ=250 |画像説明 = 山門 |所在地 = [[香川県]][[善通寺市]]弘田町1765番地1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|13|59.5|N|133|45|56.75|E|type:landmark_region:JP-37|display=inline,title}} |山号 = 医王山 |院号 = 多宝院 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗善通寺派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = （伝）[[弘仁]]12年（[[821年]]） |開山 = |開基 = （伝）[[空海]]（弘法大師） |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 医王山 多宝院 甲山寺 |別称 = |札所等 = [[四国八十八箇所]]74番 |文化財 = |公式HP = https://koyamaji.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = [香川] 医王山 多宝院 甲山寺 |地図2={{Switcher | {{Location map+|Japan Kagawa|width=230|caption= |float=center|places= {{Location map~|Japan Kagawa|lat_deg=34|lat_min=13|lat_sec=59.5|lon_deg=133|lon_min=45|lon_sec=56.75|mark=Indian_Swastika.svg|marksize=12|position=left|label=甲山寺|label_size=100|caption=}} {{Location map~|Japan Kagawa|lat_deg=34|lat_min=21|lat_sec=2.46|lon_deg=134|lon_min=2|lon_sec=48.98|mark=blue pog.svg|position=top|label=[[高松駅 (香川県)|高松駅]]|label_size=95}} {{Location map~|Japan Kagawa|lat_deg=34|lat_min=12|lat_sec=51|lon_deg=134|lon_min=0|lon_sec=56|mark=BSicon FLUGg.svg|marksize=10|position=bottom|label=[[高松空港]]|label_size=95}}}} | 香川県における位置 | {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=230}} | 善通寺市内での位置 }} }} '''甲山寺'''（こうやまじ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]にある[[真言宗善通寺派]]の[[寺院]]。医王山、院号は多宝院と号す。[[本尊]]は[[薬師如来]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]第七十四番[[札所]]。[[甲山 (香川県)|甲山]]（かぶとやま）の麓に位置する。 * 本尊[[真言]]：おん ころころ せんだりまとうぎ そわか * [[ご詠歌]]：十二神（じゅうにしん）味方に持てる戦（いくさ）には おのれとこころ甲（かぶと）やまかな * 納経印：当寺本尊、多聞天堂毘沙門天、うさぎの朱印（200円）
== 概要 == [[File:Kouyamaji20210309 1.jpg|170px|left|thumb|甲山]] 伝説では、壮年期の空海が寺を建立しようと幼きとき遊んだこのあたりを探索していると老翁が現れ、この地にお寺を建立すべしとお告げをした。この地の岩窟に住む毘沙門天の化身と悟った空海はこの岩窟に毘沙門天を祀った。 その後、嵯峨天皇の勅命を受け別当として[[満濃池]]の修築を命じられた空海は、この地で薬師如来を刻み修法すると数万の人々が集まり、無事に築造を完成に導いた。[[弘仁]]12年（[[821年]]）その功績に対して朝廷から二万銭が与えられ、その一部によって堂を建立したのが当寺の始まりであるという。
天正年間の兵火により本尊とわずかの寺宝が残ったのみで、『四国徧礼霊場記』（1689年刊）には、「むかし大伽藍の所といえども荒涼せり盛衰は世の数ある・・」と記されている状況であったが、享保20年（1730）に本堂、寛保2年（1742）に大師堂が再建され、『四国遍礼名所図会』（1800年刊）の挿絵では現在の形に近い形に復興している。
甲山（標高87.2 m)には、室町時代に天霧城の出城があったと云われていて、山頂に石舞台跡があり、頂上には神武天皇孝明天皇震儀石がある。また、当寺から山頂に向かう山道には江戸時代末期に開かれたという西国三十三所の石仏が並んでいる<ref>現地案内看板より</ref>。
== 伽藍 == [[File:Kouyamaji kyuusannmonn1.jpg|100px|thumb|旧山門]] [[File:Kouyamaji bisyamongutu1.jpg|100px|thumb|旧毘沙門窟]] * [[山門]]：2008年落慶 * [[中門]]：明和6年（1769）に完成の旧山門を中門として修築。 * [[本堂]]：享保20年（1735）完成。本尊を拝観できる(脱履物）。本尊は薬師如来坐像（肥松の一木造り、彫眼）、脇仏は日光月光菩薩立像、右脇陣に毘沙門天立像、左脇陣に阿弥陀如来坐像。 * [[大師堂]]：寛保2年（1742）完成。黒衣の大師像で、2014年と2020年に開帳された。 * 客殿：書道家の岡本光平氏が襖に描いた般若心経の襖書 * 護摩堂：天明2年（1782）完成。不動明王坐像と二童子。 * 多聞天堂：奥は毘沙門窟で、石を彫った[[毘沙門天]]、両脇に吉祥天と禅膩師童子が祀られている。 * 子安地蔵：大師堂に向って右前にいて、前掛けを持ち帰ると子供が生まれたというエピソードから。 * 淡島大明神（祠） * 鐘楼堂：宝暦2年（1752）完成。 * ウサギが16羽：本尊脇仏の月光菩薩の持物にウサギが描かれていることから、山門や中門の内側および茶堂の屋根と手水舎の前にいる<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |title=ようこそウサギ寺 境内に“１６匹”お守りにも 善通寺・甲山寺 |newspaper=四国新聞 |date=2020-04-03 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |accessdate=2023-07-15 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200405172931/http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |archivedate=2020-04-05 }}</ref>。 山門をくぐり右に手水舎を越え、左に曲がり中門をくぐると右手に納経所、わずかな石段を上がると本堂その右の続に護摩堂、左に数段上がると大師堂があり、鐘楼がその先にあり、その向こうに毘沙門窟入口があり、中に毘沙門天の石像が祀られている。 * [[宿坊]]：なし * 駐車場：無料大駐車場が山門前にあり * [[水神]]社（祠）：境内の一番高い所に祀られる。 * ミニ西国三十三観音めぐり：境内から山頂に向かう遊歩道に点在している。 <gallery> Kouyamaji 20201124 01.jpg|屋根瓦のウサギ Kōyama-ji, the Grounds of the Temple 001.jpg|境内 Kōyama-ji, Main Hall 001.jpg|本堂 Kouyamazi 02.jpg|大師堂 Kouyamazi 03.jpg|客殿（左）と護摩堂 Kouyamazi02.jpg|多聞天堂 Kouyamaji20210309 3.jpg|中門（旧山門） Kouyamaji 20250411 1.jpg|庭園 Kōyama-ji, Auxiliary Awashima Shrine 001.jpg|淡島大明神 Kouyamaji suizin01.jpg|水神社 </gallery>
== 文化財 == ; 国の史跡 * [[遍路道#文化財|讃岐遍路道]] 甲山寺境内 - 遍路道は、甲山北側を東へと回り込み旧山門へ至り、75番善通寺へは門前の弘田川に架けられた石橋（現在は橋は無い）を渡り南へ向かった。令和6年12月20日答申。<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref> == 交通アクセス == ; 鉄道 * [[四国旅客鉄道]]（JR四国） [[土讃線]]{{Ndash}}[[善通寺駅]] (3.6 km) ; 道路 * 一般道：[[香川県道48号善通寺詫間線]] 善通寺球場 (0.3 km) * 自動車道：[[高松自動車道]] [[善通寺インターチェンジ|善通寺IC]] (4.5 km)
== 前後の札所 == ; [[四国八十八箇所]] : 73 [[出釈迦寺]] --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-- '''74 甲山寺''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[善通寺]] == 周辺 == [[File:Asanozizou 20250411 1.jpg|150px|thumb|阿瀬の地蔵菩薩堂]] * 阿瀬の地蔵菩薩 :行基作と伝えられる像高約2m、檜の寄木造りの立像。天霧山麓から戦国時代に難を避けるため当地に移された。昭和62年7月21日市有形文化財指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=宮崎建樹 |year=2007 |title=四国遍路ひとり歩き同行二人 |volume=地図編 |edition=第8版 |publisher=へんろみち保存協力会 }}
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[日本の寺院一覧]] * [[七ヶ所まいり]]
== 外部リンク == * {{official website|koyamaji.or.jp/ |医王山 多宝院 甲山寺}}（寺院公式） * {{url|https://www.88shikokuhenro.jp/74koyamaji/ |第74番札所 医王山 多宝院 甲山寺}}（四国八十八ヶ所霊場会公式） * {{url|https://7kasyomairi.sakura.ne.jp/ |七ヶ所まいり}}（七か所まいり実行委員会公式） {{四国八十八箇所}} {{Buddhism2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうやまし}} [[Category:香川県の寺]] [[Category:真言宗善通寺派の寺院]] [[Category:四国八十八箇所]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]] [[Category:善通寺市の建築物]]

天正年間の兵火により本尊とわずかの寺宝が残ったのみで、『四国徧礼霊場記』（1689年刊）には、「むかし大伽藍の所といえども荒涼せり盛衰は世の数ある・・」と記されている状況であったが、享保20年（1730）に本堂、寛保2年（1742）に大師堂が再建され、『四国遍礼名所図会』（1800年刊）の挿絵では現在の形に近い形に復興している。
甲山（標高87.2 m)には、室町時代に天霧城の出城があったと云われていて、山頂に石舞台跡があり、頂上には神武天皇孝明天皇震儀石がある。また、当寺から山頂に向かう山道には江戸時代末期に開かれたという西国三十三所の石仏が並んでいる<ref>現地案内看板より</ref>。
== 伽藍 == [[File:Kouyamaji kyuusannmonn1.jpg|100px|thumb|旧山門]] [[File:Kouyamaji bisyamongutu1.jpg|100px|thumb|旧毘沙門窟]] * [[山門]]：2008年落慶 * [[中門]]：明和6年（1769）に完成の旧山門を中門として修築。 * [[本堂]]：享保20年（1735）完成。本尊を拝観できる(脱履物）。本尊は薬師如来坐像（肥松の一木造り、彫眼）、脇仏は日光月光菩薩立像、右脇陣に毘沙門天立像、左脇陣に阿弥陀如来坐像。 * [[大師堂]]：寛保2年（1742）完成。黒衣の大師像で、2014年と2020年に開帳された。 * 客殿：書道家の岡本光平氏が襖に描いた般若心経の襖書 * 護摩堂：天明2年（1782）完成。不動明王坐像と二童子。 * 多聞天堂：奥は毘沙門窟で、石を彫った[[毘沙門天]]、両脇に吉祥天と禅膩師童子が祀られている。 * 子安地蔵：大師堂に向って右前にいて、前掛けを持ち帰ると子供が生まれたというエピソードから。 * 淡島大明神（祠） * 鐘楼堂：宝暦2年（1752）完成。 * ウサギが16羽：本尊脇仏の月光菩薩の持物にウサギが描かれていることから、山門や中門の内側および茶堂の屋根と手水舎の前にいる<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |title=ようこそウサギ寺 境内に“１６匹”お守りにも 善通寺・甲山寺 |newspaper=四国新聞 |date=2020-04-03 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |accessdate=2023-07-15 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200405172931/http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |archivedate=2020-04-05 }}</ref>。 山門をくぐり右に手水舎を越え、左に曲がり中門をくぐると右手に納経所、わずかな石段を上がると本堂その右の続に護摩堂、左に数段上がると大師堂があり、鐘楼がその先にあり、その向こうに毘沙門窟入口があり、中に毘沙門天の石像が祀られている。 * [[宿坊]]：なし * 駐車場：無料大駐車場が山門前にあり * [[水神]]社（祠）：境内の一番高い所に祀られる。 * ミニ西国三十三観音めぐり：境内から山頂に向かう遊歩道に点在している。 <gallery> Kouyamaji 20201124 01.jpg|屋根瓦のウサギ Kōyama-ji, the Grounds of the Temple 001.jpg|境内 Kōyama-ji, Main Hall 001.jpg|本堂 Kouyamazi 02.jpg|大師堂 Kouyamazi 03.jpg|客殿（左）と護摩堂 Kouyamazi02.jpg|多聞天堂 Kouyamaji20210309 3.jpg|中門（旧山門） Kouyamaji 20250411 1.jpg|庭園 Kōyama-ji, Auxiliary Awashima Shrine 001.jpg|淡島大明神 Kouyamaji suizin01.jpg|水神社 </gallery>
== 文化財 == ; 国の史跡 * [[遍路道#文化財|讃岐遍路道]] 甲山寺境内 - 遍路道は、甲山北側を東へと回り込み旧山門へ至り、75番善通寺へは門前の弘田川に架けられた石橋（現在は橋は無い）を渡り南へ向かった。令和6年12月20日答申。<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref> == 交通アクセス == ; 鉄道 * [[四国旅客鉄道]]（JR四国） [[土讃線]]{{Ndash}}[[善通寺駅]] (3.6 km) ; 道路 * 一般道：[[香川県道48号善通寺詫間線]] 善通寺球場 (0.3 km) * 自動車道：[[高松自動車道]] [[善通寺インターチェンジ|善通寺IC]] (4.5 km)
== 前後の札所 == ; [[四国八十八箇所]] : 73 [[出釈迦寺]] --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-- '''74 甲山寺''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[善通寺]] == 周辺 == [[File:Asanozizou 20250411 1.jpg|150px|thumb|阿瀬の地蔵菩薩堂]] * 阿瀬の地蔵菩薩 :行基作と伝えられる像高約2m、檜の寄木造りの立像。天霧山麓から戦国時代に難を避けるため当地に移された。昭和62年7月21日市有形文化財指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=宮崎建樹 |year=2007 |title=四国遍路ひとり歩き同行二人 |volume=地図編 |edition=第8版 |publisher=へんろみち保存協力会 }}

Position in Kagawa Prefecture | {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=230}} | Position in Zentsūji City }} }}
'''Koyama-ji''' (甲山寺, こうやまじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|qq=|1=Shingon Sect Zentsuji School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583514}} located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]]. It is also known by its mountain name, [[:en:Iozen|Iōzan]], and by its temple name, {{ill|Taho-in|ja|多宝院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taho-in|qq=|1=Taho-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16282425}}. The principal image is [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], and it is the 74th temple on the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] pilgrimage. The temple is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (also called Kabutoyama).
* The mantra of the principal image is: "on korokoro sendarimātōgi sowaka" (おん ころころ せんだりまとうぎ そわか). * The temple hymn (ご詠歌) is: "In battles where the Twelve Deities (十二神) are my allies, may my heart be like Koyama" (十二神味方に持てる戦には おのれとこころ甲やまかな). * The Nōkyō seal includes the temple’s principal image, the guardian deity {{ill|Tamonten|ja|多聞天堂毘沙門天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamonten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a rabbit seal (worth 200 yen).
== Overview == {{File:Kouyamaji20210309 1.jpg|170px|left|thumb|Koyama-ji}} According to legend, during his prime, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] once explored this area—where he had played as a child—and encountered an old man who prophesied that a temple should be built here. Believing the old man to be an incarnation of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] who dwelled in a local cave, Kūkai enshrined Bishamonten in that cave. Later, upon receiving an imperial edict from Emperor [[:en:Emperor Saga|Saga]] to rebuild [[:en:Manno Lake|Mannō-ike]], Kūkai inscribed an image of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] and conducted rituals that drew tens of thousands of people, leading to the successful Completion tier 1]] == Kōyama-ji == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] : 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74 Kōyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]
== Notes ==

== Bibliography == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=宮崎建樹 |year=2007 |title=四国遍路ひとり歩き同行二人 |volume=地図編 |edition=第8版 |publisher=へんろみち保存協力会 }}

{{Buddhism-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|甲山寺|version=104504390|comment=Imported full ja history}}
(821), the imperial court awarded 20,000 sen in recognition of his achievements, a portion of which was used to construct the temple, marking its founding.
During the battles of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] period, only the principal image and a few temple treasures survived. The "Shikoku Henrei Reishōki" (published in 1689) records that "even though it was once a grand temple, it had fallen into ruin—as is the fate of many things." However, the main hall was rebuilt in the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] (1730), and the Daisidō (Great Master Hall) was rebuilt in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]] (1742). An illustration in the "Shikoku Henrei Meisho Zukai" (published in 1800) shows a form close to the present one.
On [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (elevation 87.2 m), it is said that during the Muromachi period an outpost of [[:en:Amagiri Castle|Amagiri Castle]] once stood, evidenced by a stone stage on the summit and the presence of a commemorative stone dedicated to Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]] and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Kōmyō]]. In addition, along the mountain path from the temple to the summit, stone Buddhas—dating from the late Edo period and part of the [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]—are arrayed.<ref>According to local signage.</ref>
== Temple Complex == {{File:Kouyamaji kyuusannmonn1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Main Gate}} {{File:Kouyamaji bisyamongutu1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Bishamonten Cave}} * **Main Gate:** Fell into disrepair in 2008. * **Middle Gate:** Rebuilt in 1769 (the 6th year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]) as the middle gate. * **Main Hall:** Completed in 1735 (the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]]). Visitors are required to remove their footwear to view the principal image. The principal image is a seated statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (a monolithic carving from Hiwamatsu with carved eyes); the attendant Buddhas include a standing statue of {{ill|Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|ja|日光月光菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a standing statue of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] on the right flank, and a seated statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] on the left. * **Daisidō (Great Master Hall):** Completed in 1742 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]]). A statue of the Great Master in black robes was unveiled in 2014 and 2020. * **Guest Hall:** Features fusuma painted with the Heart Sutra by calligrapher Kohei Okamoto. * **Goma Hall:** Completed in 1782 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]]). Houses a seated statue of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and two child attendants. * **Tsuya Jizō:** Located to the right in front of the Daisidō; according to tradition, carrying its apron is said to bring about the birth of a child. * **Awashima Daimyōjin Shrine (Hokora)** * **Bell Tower:** Completed in 1752 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]]). * **16 Rabbits:** Derived from depictions of rabbits on the accessory of the attendant Buddha [[:en:Candraprabha|Gekkō Bosatsu]] beside the principal image, rabbits are represented on the interior of the Main and Middle Gates, as well as on the roof of the tea house and in front of the chozubasho.<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |title=Welcome to Rabbit Temple – “16 Rabbits” present on the precincts, also used as talismans at Zentsūji/Koyama-ji |newspaper=Shikoku Shimbun |date=2020-04-03 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |accessdate=2023-07-15 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200405172931/http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |archivedate=2020-04-05 }}</ref> * Proceeding through the Main Gate and past the hand-washing basin to the right, turning left to pass through the Middle Gate leads to a Nōkyō (scripture hall) on the right. A short flight of stone steps then leads to the Main Hall; to its right lies the Goma Hall, and a few steps up on the left is the Daisidō. Beyond that is the Bell Tower, and further on is the entrance to the Bishamonten Cave, within which a stone statue of Bishamonten is enshrined. * **Lodging (Shukubō):** None. * **Parking:** A free large parking lot is available in front of the Main Gate. * **Suijin Shrine (Hokora):** Enshrined at the highest point of the precincts. * **Mini Nishi-koku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage:** Markers along the walking path from the precincts to the summit.
== Cultural Properties == ; National Historic Site * The pilgrimage route of the {{ill|Sanuki Pilgrimage|ja|遍路道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Pilgrimage|qq=|1=henro-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640512}} within the precincts of Koyama-ji – This route originally wound eastward around the northern part of Koyama, reached the old Main Gate, then crossed a stone bridge (now removed) over the Hirota River in front of Zentsūji, continuing south. It was recommended in a report on December 20, Reiwa 6.<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref>
== Transportation == ; **Rail** * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company (JR Shikoku)]], [[:en:Dosan Line|Tosan Line]] – [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] (3.6 km) ; **Road** * Public road: {{ill|Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|ja|香川県道48号善通寺詫間線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|qq=|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 48|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667552}} – Zentsūji Ballpark (0.3 km) * Expressway: [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]], {{ill|Zentsūji Interchange|ja|善通寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji Interchange|qq=|1=Zentsuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10923476}} (4.5 km)
== Preceding and Succeeding Pilgrimage Sites == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] : 73: {{ill|Dushakka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dushakka-ji|qq=|1=Shusshaka-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395826}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74: Koyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75: [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsūji]]

'''Koyama-ji''' (甲山寺, こうやまじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|qq=|1=Shingon Sect Zentsuji School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583514}} located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]]. It is also known by its mountain name, [[:en:Iozen|Iōzan]], and by its temple name, {{ill|Taho-in|ja|多宝院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taho-in|qq=|1=Taho-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16282425}}. The principal image is [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], and it is the 74th temple on the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] pilgrimage. The temple is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (also called Kabutoyama).
* The mantra of the principal image is: "on korokoro sendarimātōgi sowaka" (おん ころころ せんだりまとうぎ そわか). * The temple hymn (ご詠歌) is: "In battles where the Twelve Deities (十二神) are my allies, may my heart be like Koyama" (十二神味方に持てる戦には おのれとこころ甲やまかな). * The Nōkyō seal includes the temple’s principal image, the guardian deity {{ill|Tamonten|ja|多聞天堂毘沙門天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamonten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a rabbit seal (worth 200 yen).
== Overview == {{File:Kouyamaji20210309 1.jpg|170px|left|thumb|Koyama-ji}} According to legend, during his prime, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] once explored this area—where he had played as a child—and encountered an old man who prophesied that a temple should be built here. Believing the old man to be an incarnation of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] who dwelled in a local cave, Kūkai enshrined Bishamonten in that cave. Later, upon receiving an imperial edict from Emperor [[:en:Emperor Saga|Saga]] to rebuild [[:en:Manno Lake|Mannō-ike]], Kūkai inscribed an image of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] and conducted rituals that drew tens of thousands of people, leading to the successful Completion tier 1]] == Kōyama-ji == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] : 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74 Kōyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]

{{translated page|ja|甲山寺|version=104504390|comment=Imported full ja history}}
(821), the imperial court awarded 20,000 sen in recognition of his achievements, a portion of which was used to construct the temple, marking its founding.
During the battles of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] period, only the principal image and a few temple treasures survived. The "Shikoku Henrei Reishōki" (published in 1689) records that "even though it was once a grand temple, it had fallen into ruin—as is the fate of many things." However, the main hall was rebuilt in the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] (1730), and the Daisidō (Great Master Hall) was rebuilt in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]] (1742). An illustration in the "Shikoku Henrei Meisho Zukai" (published in 1800) shows a form close to the present one.
On [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (elevation 87.2 m), it is said that during the Muromachi period an outpost of [[:en:Amagiri Castle|Amagiri Castle]] once stood, evidenced by a stone stage on the summit and the presence of a commemorative stone dedicated to Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]] and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Kōmyō]]. In addition, along the mountain path from the temple to the summit, stone Buddhas—dating from the late Edo period and part of the [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]—are arrayed.<ref>According to local signage.</ref>
== Temple Complex == {{File:Kouyamaji kyuusannmonn1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Main Gate}} {{File:Kouyamaji bisyamongutu1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Bishamonten Cave}} * **Main Gate:** Fell into disrepair in 2008. * **Middle Gate:** Rebuilt in 1769 (the 6th year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]) as the middle gate. * **Main Hall:** Completed in 1735 (the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]]). Visitors are required to remove their footwear to view the principal image. The principal image is a seated statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (a monolithic carving from Hiwamatsu with carved eyes); the attendant Buddhas include a standing statue of {{ill|Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|ja|日光月光菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a standing statue of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] on the right flank, and a seated statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] on the left. * **Daisidō (Great Master Hall):** Completed in 1742 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]]). A statue of the Great Master in black robes was unveiled in 2014 and 2020. * **Guest Hall:** Features fusuma painted with the Heart Sutra by calligrapher Kohei Okamoto. * **Goma Hall:** Completed in 1782 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]]). Houses a seated statue of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and two child attendants. * **Tsuya Jizō:** Located to the right in front of the Daisidō; according to tradition, carrying its apron is said to bring about the birth of a child. * **Awashima Daimyōjin Shrine (Hokora)** * **Bell Tower:** Completed in 1752 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]]). * **16 Rabbits:** Derived from depictions of rabbits on the accessory of the attendant Buddha [[:en:Candraprabha|Gekkō Bosatsu]] beside the principal image, rabbits are represented on the interior of the Main and Middle Gates, as well as on the roof of the tea house and in front of the chozubasho.<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |title=Welcome to Rabbit Temple – “16 Rabbits” present on the precincts, also used as talismans at Zentsūji/Koyama-ji |newspaper=Shikoku Shimbun |date=2020-04-03 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |accessdate=2023-07-15 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200405172931/http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |archivedate=2020-04-05 }}</ref> * Proceeding through the Main Gate and past the hand-washing basin to the right, turning left to pass through the Middle Gate leads to a Nōkyō (scripture hall) on the right. A short flight of stone steps then leads to the Main Hall; to its right lies the Goma Hall, and a few steps up on the left is the Daisidō. Beyond that is the Bell Tower, and further on is the entrance to the Bishamonten Cave, within which a stone statue of Bishamonten is enshrined. * **Lodging (Shukubō):** None. * **Parking:** A free large parking lot is available in front of the Main Gate. * **Suijin Shrine (Hokora):** Enshrined at the highest point of the precincts. * **Mini Nishi-koku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage:** Markers along the walking path from the precincts to the summit.
== Cultural Properties == ; National Historic Site * The pilgrimage route of the {{ill|Sanuki Pilgrimage|ja|遍路道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Pilgrimage|qq=|1=henro-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640512}} within the precincts of Koyama-ji – This route originally wound eastward around the northern part of Koyama, reached the old Main Gate, then crossed a stone bridge (now removed) over the Hirota River in front of Zentsūji, continuing south. It was recommended in a report on December 20, Reiwa 6.<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref>
== Transportation == ; **Rail** * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company (JR Shikoku)]], [[:en:Dosan Line|Tosan Line]] – [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] (3.6 km) ; **Road** * Public road: {{ill|Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|ja|香川県道48号善通寺詫間線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|qq=|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 48|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667552}} – Zentsūji Ballpark (0.3 km) * Expressway: [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]], {{ill|Zentsūji Interchange|ja|善通寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji Interchange|qq=|1=Zentsuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10923476}} (4.5 km)

'''Koyama-ji''' (甲山寺, こうやまじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|qq=|1=Shingon Sect Zentsuji School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583514}} located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]]. It is also known by its mountain name, [[:en:Iozen|Iōzan]], and by its temple name, {{ill|Taho-in|ja|多宝院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taho-in|qq=|1=Taho-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16282425}}. The principal image is [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], and it is the 74th temple on the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] pilgrimage. The temple is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (also called Kabutoyama).
* The mantra of the principal image is: "on korokoro sendarimātōgi sowaka" (おん ころころ せんだりまとうぎ そわか). * The temple hymn (ご詠歌) is: "In battles where the Twelve Deities (十二神) are my allies, may my heart be like Koyama" (十二神味方に持てる戦には おのれとこころ甲やまかな). * The Nōkyō seal includes the temple’s principal image, the guardian deity {{ill|Tamonten|ja|多聞天堂毘沙門天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamonten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a rabbit seal (worth 200 yen).
== Overview == {{File:Kouyamaji20210309 1.jpg|170px|left|thumb|Koyama-ji}} According to legend, during his prime, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] once explored this area—where he had played as a child—and encountered an old man who prophesied that a temple should be built here. Believing the old man to be an incarnation of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] who dwelled in a local cave, Kūkai enshrined Bishamonten in that cave. Later, upon receiving an imperial edict from Emperor [[:en:Emperor Saga|Saga]] to rebuild [[:en:Manno Lake|Mannō-ike]], Kūkai inscribed an image of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] and conducted rituals that drew tens of thousands of people, leading to the successful Completion tier 1]] == Kōyama-ji == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] : 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74 Kōyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]

{{translated page|ja|甲山寺|version=104504390|comment=Imported full ja history}}
(821), the imperial court awarded 20,000 sen in recognition of his achievements, a portion of which was used to construct the temple, marking its founding.
During the battles of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] period, only the principal image and a few temple treasures survived. The "Shikoku Henrei Reishōki" (published in 1689) records that "even though it was once a grand temple, it had fallen into ruin—as is the fate of many things." However, the main hall was rebuilt in the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] (1730), and the Daisidō (Great Master Hall) was rebuilt in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]] (1742). An illustration in the "Shikoku Henrei Meisho Zukai" (published in 1800) shows a form close to the present one.
On [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (elevation 87.2 m), it is said that during the Muromachi period an outpost of [[:en:Amagiri Castle|Amagiri Castle]] once stood, evidenced by a stone stage on the summit and the presence of a commemorative stone dedicated to Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]] and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Kōmyō]]. In addition, along the mountain path from the temple to the summit, stone Buddhas—dating from the late Edo period and part of the [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]—are arrayed.<ref>According to local signage.</ref>
== Temple Complex == {{File:Kouyamaji kyuusannmonn1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Main Gate}} {{File:Kouyamaji bisyamongutu1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Bishamonten Cave}} * **Main Gate:** Fell into disrepair in 2008. * **Middle Gate:** Rebuilt in 1769 (the 6th year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]) as the middle gate. * **Main Hall:** Completed in 1735 (the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]]). Visitors are required to remove their footwear to view the principal image. The principal image is a seated statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (a monolithic carving from Hiwamatsu with carved eyes); the attendant Buddhas include a standing statue of {{ill|Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|ja|日光月光菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a standing statue of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] on the right flank, and a seated statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] on the left. * **Daisidō (Great Master Hall):** Completed in 1742 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]]). A statue of the Great Master in black robes was unveiled in 2014 and 2020. * **Guest Hall:** Features fusuma painted with the Heart Sutra by calligrapher Kohei Okamoto. * **Goma Hall:** Completed in 1782 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]]). Houses a seated statue of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and two child attendants. * **Tsuya Jizō:** Located to the right in front of the Daisidō; according to tradition, carrying its apron is said to bring about the birth of a child. * **Awashima Daimyōjin Shrine (Hokora)** * **Bell Tower:** Completed in 1752 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]]). * **16 Rabbits:** Derived from depictions of rabbits on the accessory of the attendant Buddha [[:en:Candraprabha|Gekkō Bosatsu]] beside the principal image, rabbits are represented on the interior of the Main and Middle Gates, as well as on the roof of the tea house and in front of the chozubasho.<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |title=Welcome to Rabbit Temple – “16 Rabbits” present on the precincts, also used as talismans at Zentsūji/Koyama-ji |newspaper=Shikoku Shimbun |date=2020-04-03 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |accessdate=2023-07-15 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200405172931/http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |archivedate=2020-04-05 }}</ref> * Proceeding through the Main Gate and past the hand-washing basin to the right, turning left to pass through the Middle Gate leads to a Nōkyō (scripture hall) on the right. A short flight of stone steps then leads to the Main Hall; to its right lies the Goma Hall, and a few steps up on the left is the Daisidō. Beyond that is the Bell Tower, and further on is the entrance to the Bishamonten Cave, within which a stone statue of Bishamonten is enshrined. * **Lodging (Shukubō):** None. * **Parking:** A free large parking lot is available in front of the Main Gate. * **Suijin Shrine (Hokora):** Enshrined at the highest point of the precincts. * **Mini Nishi-koku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage:** Markers along the walking path from the precincts to the summit.
== Cultural Properties == ; National Historic Site * The pilgrimage route of the {{ill|Sanuki Pilgrimage|ja|遍路道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Pilgrimage|qq=|1=henro-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640512}} within the precincts of Koyama-ji – This route originally wound eastward around the northern part of Koyama, reached the old Main Gate, then crossed a stone bridge (now removed) over the Hirota River in front of Zentsūji, continuing south. It was recommended in a report on December 20, Reiwa 6.<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref>
== Transportation == ; **Rail** * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company (JR Shikoku)]], [[:en:Dosan Line|Tosan Line]] – [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] (3.6 km) ; **Road** * Public road: {{ill|Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|ja|香川県道48号善通寺詫間線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|qq=|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 48|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667552}} – Zentsūji Ballpark (0.3 km) * Expressway: [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]], {{ill|Zentsūji Interchange|ja|善通寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji Interchange|qq=|1=Zentsuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10923476}} (4.5 km)

Position in Kagawa Prefecture | {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=230}} | Position in Zentsūji City }} }}
'''Koyama-ji''' (甲山寺, こうやまじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|qq=|1=Shingon Sect Zentsuji School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583514}} located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]]. It is also known by its mountain name, [[:en:Iozen|Iōzan]], and by its temple name, {{ill|Taho-in|ja|多宝院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taho-in|qq=|1=Taho-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16282425}}. The principal image is [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], and it is the 74th temple on the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] pilgrimage. The temple is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (also called Kabutoyama).
* The mantra of the principal image is: "on korokoro sendarimātōgi sowaka" (おん ころころ せんだりまとうぎ そわか). * The temple hymn (ご詠歌) is: "In battles where the Twelve Deities (十二神) are my allies, may my heart be like Koyama" (十二神味方に持てる戦には おのれとこころ甲やまかな). * The Nōkyō seal includes the temple’s principal image, the guardian deity {{ill|Tamonten|ja|多聞天堂毘沙門天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamonten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a rabbit seal (worth 200 yen).
== Overview == {{File:Kouyamaji20210309 1.jpg|170px|left|thumb|Koyama-ji}} According to legend, during his prime, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] once explored this area—where he had played as a child—and encountered an old man who prophesied that a temple should be built here. Believing the old man to be an incarnation of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] who dwelled in a local cave, Kūkai enshrined Bishamonten in that cave. Later, upon receiving an imperial edict from Emperor [[:en:Emperor Saga|Saga]] to rebuild [[:en:Manno Lake|Mannō-ike]], Kūkai inscribed an image of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] and conducted rituals that drew tens of thousands of people, leading to the successful Completion tier 1]] == Kōyama-ji == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] : 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74 Kōyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]

'''Koyama-ji''' (甲山寺, こうやまじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|1=Shingon Sect Zentsuji School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583514}} located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]]. It is also known by its mountain name, [[:en:Iozen|Iōzan]], and by its temple name, {{ill|Taho-in|ja|多宝院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taho-in|1=Taho-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16282425}}. The principal image is [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], and it is the 74th temple on the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] pilgrimage. The temple is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (also called Kabutoyama).
* The mantra of the principal image is: "on korokoro sendarimātōgi sowaka" (おん ころころ せんだりまとうぎ そわか). * The temple hymn (ご詠歌) is: "In battles where the Twelve Deities (十二神) are my allies, may my heart be like Koyama" (十二神味方に持てる戦には おのれとこころ甲やまかな). * The Nōkyō seal includes the temple’s principal image, the guardian deity {{ill|Tamonten|ja|多聞天堂毘沙門天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamonten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a rabbit seal (worth 200 yen).
== Overview == {{File:Kouyamaji20210309 1.jpg|170px|left|thumb|Koyama-ji}} According to legend, during his prime, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] once explored this area—where he had played as a child—and encountered an old man who prophesied that a temple should be built here. Believing the old man to be an incarnation of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] who dwelled in a local cave, Kūkai enshrined Bishamonten in that cave. Later, upon receiving an imperial edict from Emperor [[:en:Emperor Saga|Saga]] to rebuild [[:en:Manno Lake|Mannō-ike]], Kūkai inscribed an image of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] and conducted rituals that drew tens of thousands of people, leading to the successful Completion tier 1]] == Kōyama-ji == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] : 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74 Kōyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]

{{translated page|ja|甲山寺|version=104504390|comment=Imported full ja history}}
(821), the imperial court awarded 20,000 sen in recognition of his achievements, a portion of which was used to construct the temple, marking its founding.
During the battles of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] period, only the principal image and a few temple treasures survived. The "Shikoku Henrei Reishōki" (published in 1689) records that "even though it was once a grand temple, it had fallen into ruin—as is the fate of many things." However, the main hall was rebuilt in the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] (1730), and the Daisidō (Great Master Hall) was rebuilt in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]] (1742). An illustration in the "Shikoku Henrei Meisho Zukai" (published in 1800) shows a form close to the present one.
On [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (elevation 87.2 m), it is said that during the Muromachi period an outpost of [[:en:Amagiri Castle|Amagiri Castle]] once stood, evidenced by a stone stage on the summit and the presence of a commemorative stone dedicated to Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]] and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Kōmyō]]. In addition, along the mountain path from the temple to the summit, stone Buddhas—dating from the late Edo period and part of the [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]—are arrayed.<ref>According to local signage.</ref>
== Temple Complex == {{File:Kouyamaji kyuusannmonn1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Main Gate}} {{File:Kouyamaji bisyamongutu1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Bishamonten Cave}} * **Main Gate:** Fell into disrepair in 2008. * **Middle Gate:** Rebuilt in 1769 (the 6th year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]) as the middle gate. * **Main Hall:** Completed in 1735 (the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]]). Visitors are required to remove their footwear to view the principal image. The principal image is a seated statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (a monolithic carving from Hiwamatsu with carved eyes); the attendant Buddhas include a standing statue of {{ill|Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|ja|日光月光菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a standing statue of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] on the right flank, and a seated statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] on the left. * **Daisidō (Great Master Hall):** Completed in 1742 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]]). A statue of the Great Master in black robes was unveiled in 2014 and 2020. * **Guest Hall:** Features fusuma painted with the Heart Sutra by calligrapher Kohei Okamoto. * **Goma Hall:** Completed in 1782 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]]). Houses a seated statue of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and two child attendants. * **Tsuya Jizō:** Located to the right in front of the Daisidō; according to tradition, carrying its apron is said to bring about the birth of a child. * **Awashima Daimyōjin Shrine (Hokora)** * **Bell Tower:** Completed in 1752 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]]). * **16 Rabbits:** Derived from depictions of rabbits on the accessory of the attendant Buddha [[:en:Candraprabha|Gekkō Bosatsu]] beside the principal image, rabbits are represented on the interior of the Main and Middle Gates, as well as on the roof of the tea house and in front of the chozubasho.<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |title=Welcome to Rabbit Temple – “16 Rabbits” present on the precincts, also used as talismans at Zentsūji/Koyama-ji |newspaper=Shikoku Shimbun |date=2020-04-03 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |accessdate=2023-07-15 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200405172931/http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |archivedate=2020-04-05 }}</ref> * Proceeding through the Main Gate and past the hand-washing basin to the right, turning left to pass through the Middle Gate leads to a Nōkyō (scripture hall) on the right. A short flight of stone steps then leads to the Main Hall; to its right lies the Goma Hall, and a few steps up on the left is the Daisidō. Beyond that is the Bell Tower, and further on is the entrance to the Bishamonten Cave, within which a stone statue of Bishamonten is enshrined. * **Lodging (Shukubō):** None. * **Parking:** A free large parking lot is available in front of the Main Gate. * **Suijin Shrine (Hokora):** Enshrined at the highest point of the precincts. * **Mini Nishi-koku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage:** Markers along the walking path from the precincts to the summit.
== Cultural Properties == ; National Historic Site * The pilgrimage route of the {{ill|Sanuki Pilgrimage|ja|遍路道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Pilgrimage|1=henro-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640512}} within the precincts of Koyama-ji – This route originally wound eastward around the northern part of Koyama, reached the old Main Gate, then crossed a stone bridge (now removed) over the Hirota River in front of Zentsūji, continuing south. It was recommended in a report on December 20, Reiwa 6.<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref>

Position in Kagawa Prefecture | {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=230}} | Position in Zentsūji City }} }}
'''Koyama-ji''' (甲山寺, こうやまじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|1=Shingon Sect Zentsuji School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583514}} located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]]. It is also known by its mountain name, [[:en:Iozen|Iōzan]], and by its temple name, {{ill|Taho-in|ja|多宝院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taho-in|1=Taho-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16282425}}. The principal image is [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], and it is the 74th temple on the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] pilgrimage. The temple is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (also called Kabutoyama).
* The mantra of the principal image is: "on korokoro sendarimātōgi sowaka" (おん ころころ せんだりまとうぎ そわか). * The temple hymn (ご詠歌) is: "In battles where the Twelve Deities (十二神) are my allies, may my heart be like Koyama" (十二神味方に持てる戦には おのれとこころ甲やまかな). * The Nōkyō seal includes the temple’s principal image, the guardian deity {{ill|Tamonten|ja|多聞天堂毘沙門天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamonten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a rabbit seal (worth 200 yen).
== Overview == {{File:Kouyamaji20210309 1.jpg|170px|left|thumb|Koyama-ji}} According to legend, during his prime, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] once explored this area—where he had played as a child—and encountered an old man who prophesied that a temple should be built here. Believing the old man to be an incarnation of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] who dwelled in a local cave, Kūkai enshrined Bishamonten in that cave. Later, upon receiving an imperial edict from Emperor [[:en:Emperor Saga|Saga]] to rebuild [[:en:Manno Lake|Mannō-ike]], Kūkai inscribed an image of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] and conducted rituals that drew tens of thousands of people, leading to the successful Completion tier 1]] == Kōyama-ji == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] : 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74 Kōyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]

{{translated page|ja|甲山寺|version=104504390|comment=Imported full ja history}}
(821), the imperial court awarded 20,000 sen in recognition of his achievements, a portion of which was used to construct the temple, marking its founding.
During the battles of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] period, only the principal image and a few temple treasures survived. The "Shikoku Henrei Reishōki" (published in 1689) records that "even though it was once a grand temple, it had fallen into ruin—as is the fate of many things." However, the main hall was rebuilt in the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] (1730), and the Daisidō (Great Master Hall) was rebuilt in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]] (1742). An illustration in the "Shikoku Henrei Meisho Zukai" (published in 1800) shows a form close to the present one.
On [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (elevation 87.2 m), it is said that during the Muromachi period an outpost of [[:en:Amagiri Castle|Amagiri Castle]] once stood, evidenced by a stone stage on the summit and the presence of a commemorative stone dedicated to Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]] and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Kōmyō]]. In addition, along the mountain path from the temple to the summit, stone Buddhas—dating from the late Edo period and part of the [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]—are arrayed.<ref>According to local signage.</ref>
== Temple Complex == {{File:Kouyamaji kyuusannmonn1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Main Gate}} {{File:Kouyamaji bisyamongutu1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Bishamonten Cave}} * **Main Gate:** Fell into disrepair in 2008. * **Middle Gate:** Rebuilt in 1769 (the 6th year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]) as the middle gate. * **Main Hall:** Completed in 1735 (the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]]). Visitors are required to remove their footwear to view the principal image. The principal image is a seated statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (a monolithic carving from Hiwamatsu with carved eyes); the attendant Buddhas include a standing statue of {{ill|Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|ja|日光月光菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a standing statue of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] on the right flank, and a seated statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] on the left. * **Daisidō (Great Master Hall):** Completed in 1742 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]]). A statue of the Great Master in black robes was unveiled in 2014 and 2020. * **Guest Hall:** Features fusuma painted with the Heart Sutra by calligrapher Kohei Okamoto. * **Goma Hall:** Completed in 1782 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]]). Houses a seated statue of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and two child attendants. * **Tsuya Jizō:** Located to the right in front of the Daisidō; according to tradition, carrying its apron is said to bring about the birth of a child. * **Awashima Daimyōjin Shrine (Hokora)** * **Bell Tower:** Completed in 1752 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]]). * **16 Rabbits:** Derived from depictions of rabbits on the accessory of the attendant Buddha [[:en:Candraprabha|Gekkō Bosatsu]] beside the principal image, rabbits are represented on the interior of the Main and Middle Gates, as well as on the roof of the tea house and in front of the chozubasho.<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |title=Welcome to Rabbit Temple – “16 Rabbits” present on the precincts, also used as talismans at Zentsūji/Koyama-ji |newspaper=Shikoku Shimbun |date=2020-04-03 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |accessdate=2023-07-15 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200405172931/http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |archivedate=2020-04-05 }}</ref> * Proceeding through the Main Gate and past the hand-washing basin to the right, turning left to pass through the Middle Gate leads to a Nōkyō (scripture hall) on the right. A short flight of stone steps then leads to the Main Hall; to its right lies the Goma Hall, and a few steps up on the left is the Daisidō. Beyond that is the Bell Tower, and further on is the entrance to the Bishamonten Cave, within which a stone statue of Bishamonten is enshrined. * **Lodging (Shukubō):** None. * **Parking:** A free large parking lot is available in front of the Main Gate. * **Suijin Shrine (Hokora):** Enshrined at the highest point of the precincts. * **Mini Nishi-koku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage:** Markers along the walking path from the precincts to the summit.
== Cultural Properties == ; National Historic Site * The pilgrimage route of the {{ill|Sanuki Pilgrimage|ja|遍路道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Pilgrimage|1=henro-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640512}} within the precincts of Koyama-ji – This route originally wound eastward around the northern part of Koyama, reached the old Main Gate, then crossed a stone bridge (now removed) over the Hirota River in front of Zentsūji, continuing south. It was recommended in a report on December 20, Reiwa 6.<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref>
== Transportation == ; **Rail** * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company (JR Shikoku)]], [[:en:Dosan Line|Tosan Line]] – [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] (3.6 km) ; **Road** * Public road: {{ill|Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|ja|香川県道48号善通寺詫間線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 48|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667552}} – Zentsūji Ballpark (0.3 km) * Expressway: [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]], {{ill|Zentsūji Interchange|ja|善通寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji Interchange|1=Zentsuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10923476}} (4.5 km)
== Preceding and Succeeding Pilgrimage Sites == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] : 73: {{ill|Dushakka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dushakka-ji|1=Shusshaka-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395826}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74: Koyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75: [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsūji]]

'''Koyama-ji''' (甲山寺, こうやまじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|1=Shingon Sect Zentsuji School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583514}} located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]]. It is also known by its mountain name, [[:en:Iozen|Iōzan]], and by its temple name, {{ill|Taho-in|ja|多宝院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taho-in|1=Taho-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16282425}}. The principal image is [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], and it is the 74th temple on the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] pilgrimage. The temple is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (also called Kabutoyama).
* The mantra of the principal image is: "on korokoro sendarimātōgi sowaka" (おん ころころ せんだりまとうぎ そわか). * The temple hymn (ご詠歌) is: "In battles where the Twelve Deities (十二神) are my allies, may my heart be like Koyama" (十二神味方に持てる戦には おのれとこころ甲やまかな). * The Nōkyō seal includes the temple’s principal image, the guardian deity {{ill|Tamonten|ja|多聞天堂毘沙門天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamonten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a rabbit seal (worth 200 yen).

Believing the old man to be an incarnation of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] who dwelled in a local cave, Kūkai enshrined Bishamonten in that cave. Later, upon receiving an imperial edict from Emperor [[:en:Emperor Saga|Saga]] to rebuild [[:en:Manno Lake|Mannō-ike]], Kūkai inscribed an image of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] and conducted rituals that drew tens of thousands of people, leading to the successful Completion tier 1]] == Kōyama-ji == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] : 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74 Kōyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]

(821), the imperial court awarded 20,000 sen in recognition of his achievements, a portion of which was used to construct the temple, marking its founding.

Beyond that is the Bell Tower, and further on is the entrance to the Bishamonten Cave, within which a stone statue of Bishamonten is enshrined. * **Lodging (Shukubō):** None. * **Parking:** A free large parking lot is available in front of the Main Gate. * **Suijin Shrine (Hokora):** Enshrined at the highest point of the precincts. * **Mini Nishi-koku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage:** Markers along the walking path from the precincts to the summit.
== Cultural Properties == ; National Historic Site * The pilgrimage route of the {{ill|Sanuki Pilgrimage|ja|遍路道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Pilgrimage|1=henro-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640512}} within the precincts of Koyama-ji – This route originally wound eastward around the northern part of Koyama, reached the old Main Gate, then crossed a stone bridge (now removed) over the Hirota River in front of Zentsūji, continuing south. It was recommended in a report on December 20, Reiwa 6.<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref>
== Transportation == ; **Rail** * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company (JR Shikoku)]], [[:en:Dosan Line|Tosan Line]] – [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] (3.6 km) ; **Road** * Public road: {{ill|Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|ja|香川県道48号善通寺詫間線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 48|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667552}} – Zentsūji Ballpark (0.3 km) * Expressway: [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]], {{ill|Zentsūji Interchange|ja|善通寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji Interchange|1=Zentsuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10923476}} (4.5 km)

Position in Kagawa Prefecture | {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=230}} | Position in Zentsūji City }} }}
'''Koyama-ji''' (甲山寺, こうやまじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|1=Shingon Sect Zentsuji School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583514}} located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]]. It is also known by its mountain name, [[:en:Iozen|Iōzan]], and by its temple name, {{ill|Taho-in|ja|多宝院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taho-in|1=Taho-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16282425}}. The principal image is [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], and it is the 74th temple on the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] pilgrimage. The temple is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (also called Kabutoyama).
* The mantra of the principal image is: "on korokoro sendarimātōgi sowaka" (おん ころころ せんだりまとうぎ そわか). * The temple hymn (ご詠歌) is: "In battles where the Twelve Deities (十二神) are my allies, may my heart be like Koyama" (十二神味方に持てる戦には おのれとこころ甲やまかな). * The Nōkyō seal includes the temple’s principal image, the guardian deity {{ill|Tamonten|ja|多聞天堂毘沙門天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamonten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a rabbit seal (worth 200 yen).
== Overview == {{File:Kouyamaji20210309 1.jpg|170px|left|thumb|Koyama-ji}} According to legend, during his prime, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] once explored this area—where he had played as a child—and encountered an old man who prophesied that a temple should be built here. Believing the old man to be an incarnation of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] who dwelled in a local cave, Kūkai enshrined Bishamonten in that cave. Later, upon receiving an imperial edict from Emperor [[:en:Emperor Saga|Saga]] to rebuild [[:en:Manno Lake|Mannō-ike]], Kūkai inscribed an image of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] and conducted rituals that drew tens of thousands of people, leading to the successful Completion tier 1]] == Kōyama-ji == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] : 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74 Kōyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]

{{Buddhism-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|甲山寺|version=104504390|comment=Imported full ja history}}
(821), the imperial court awarded 20,000 sen in recognition of his achievements, a portion of which was used to construct the temple, marking its founding.
During the battles of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] period, only the principal image and a few temple treasures survived. The "Shikoku Henrei Reishōki" (published in 1689) records that "even though it was once a grand temple, it had fallen into ruin—as is the fate of many things." However, the main hall was rebuilt in the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] (1730), and the Daisidō (Great Master Hall) was rebuilt in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]] (1742). An illustration in the "Shikoku Henrei Meisho Zukai" (published in 1800) shows a form close to the present one.
On [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (elevation 87.2 m), it is said that during the Muromachi period an outpost of [[:en:Amagiri Castle|Amagiri Castle]] once stood, evidenced by a stone stage on the summit and the presence of a commemorative stone dedicated to Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]] and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Kōmyō]]. In addition, along the mountain path from the temple to the summit, stone Buddhas—dating from the late Edo period and part of the [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]—are arrayed.<ref>According to local signage.</ref>
== Temple Complex == {{File:Kouyamaji kyuusannmonn1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Main Gate}} {{File:Kouyamaji bisyamongutu1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Bishamonten Cave}} * **Main Gate:** Fell into disrepair in 2008. * **Middle Gate:** Rebuilt in 1769 (the 6th year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]) as the middle gate. * **Main Hall:** Completed in 1735 (the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]]). Visitors are required to remove their footwear to view the principal image. The principal image is a seated statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (a monolithic carving from Hiwamatsu with carved eyes); the attendant Buddhas include a standing statue of {{ill|Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|ja|日光月光菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a standing statue of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] on the right flank, and a seated statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] on the left. * **Daisidō (Great Master Hall):** Completed in 1742 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]]). A statue of the Great Master in black robes was unveiled in 2014 and 2020. * **Guest Hall:** Features fusuma painted with the Heart Sutra by calligrapher Kohei Okamoto. * **Goma Hall:** Completed in 1782 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]]). Houses a seated statue of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and two child attendants. * **Tsuya Jizō:** Located to the right in front of the Daisidō; according to tradition, carrying its apron is said to bring about the birth of a child. * **Awashima Daimyōjin Shrine (Hokora)** * **Bell Tower:** Completed in 1752 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]]). * **16 Rabbits:** Derived from depictions of rabbits on the accessory of the attendant Buddha [[:en:Candraprabha|Gekkō Bosatsu]] beside the principal image, rabbits are represented on the interior of the Main and Middle Gates, as well as on the roof of the tea house and in front of the chozubasho.<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |title=Welcome to Rabbit Temple – “16 Rabbits” present on the precincts, also used as talismans at Zentsūji/Koyama-ji |newspaper=Shikoku Shimbun |date=2020-04-03 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |accessdate=2023-07-15 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200405172931/http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |archivedate=2020-04-05 }}</ref> * Proceeding through the Main Gate and past the hand-washing basin to the right, turning left to pass through the Middle Gate leads to a Nōkyō (scripture hall) on the right. A short flight of stone steps then leads to the Main Hall; to its right lies the Goma Hall, and a few steps up on the left is the Daisidō. Beyond that is the Bell Tower, and further on is the entrance to the Bishamonten Cave, within which a stone statue of Bishamonten is enshrined. * **Lodging (Shukubō):** None. * **Parking:** A free large parking lot is available in front of the Main Gate. * **Suijin Shrine (Hokora):** Enshrined at the highest point of the precincts. * **Mini Nishi-koku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage:** Markers along the walking path from the precincts to the summit.
== Cultural Properties == ; National Historic Site * The pilgrimage route of the {{ill|Sanuki Pilgrimage|ja|遍路道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Pilgrimage|1=henro-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640512}} within the precincts of Koyama-ji – This route originally wound eastward around the northern part of Koyama, reached the old Main Gate, then crossed a stone bridge (now removed) over the Hirota River in front of Zentsūji, continuing south. It was recommended in a report on December 20, Reiwa 6.<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref>
== Transportation == ; **Rail** * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company (JR Shikoku)]], [[:en:Dosan Line|Tosan Line]] – [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] (3.6 km) ; **Road** * Public road: {{ill|Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|ja|香川県道48号善通寺詫間線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 48|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667552}} – Zentsūji Ballpark (0.3 km) * Expressway: [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]], {{ill|Zentsūji Interchange|ja|善通寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji Interchange|1=Zentsuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10923476}} (4.5 km)
== Preceding and Succeeding Pilgrimage Sites == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] : 73: {{ill|Dushakka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dushakka-ji|1=Shusshaka-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395826}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74: Koyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75: [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsūji]]

Position in Kagawa Prefecture | {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=230}} | Position in Zentsūji City }} }}
'''Koyama-ji''' (甲山寺, こうやまじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Shentsū-ji Sect|1=Shingon Sect Zentsuji School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583514}} located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji City]]. It is also known by its mountain name, [[:en:Iozen|Iōzan]], and by its temple name, {{ill|Taho-in|ja|多宝院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taho-in|1=Taho-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16282425}}. The principal image is [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], and it is the 74th temple on the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] pilgrimage. The temple is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (also called Kabutoyama).
* The mantra of the principal image is: "on korokoro sendarimātōgi sowaka" (おん ころころ せんだりまとうぎ そわか). * The temple hymn (ご詠歌) is: "In battles where the Twelve Deities (十二神) are my allies, may my heart be like Koyama" (十二神味方に持てる戦には おのれとこころ甲やまかな). * The Nōkyō seal includes the temple’s principal image, the guardian deity {{ill|Tamonten|ja|多聞天堂毘沙門天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamonten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a rabbit seal (worth 200 yen).
== Overview == {{File:Kouyamaji20210309 1.jpg|170px|left|thumb|Koyama-ji}} According to legend, during his prime, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] once explored this area—where he had played as a child—and encountered an old man who prophesied that a temple should be built here. Believing the old man to be an incarnation of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] who dwelled in a local cave, Kūkai enshrined Bishamonten in that cave. Later, upon receiving an imperial edict from Emperor [[:en:Emperor Saga|Saga]] to rebuild [[:en:Manno Lake|Mannō-ike]], Kūkai inscribed an image of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] and conducted rituals that drew tens of thousands of people, leading to the successful Completion tier 1]] == Kōyama-ji == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku Pilgrimage]] : 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74 Kōyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75 [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]

{{translated page|ja|甲山寺|version=104504390|comment=Imported full ja history}}
(821), the imperial court awarded 20,000 sen in recognition of his achievements, a portion of which was used to construct the temple, marking its founding.
During the battles of the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] period, only the principal image and a few temple treasures survived. The "Shikoku Henrei Reishōki" (published in 1689) records that "even though it was once a grand temple, it had fallen into ruin—as is the fate of many things." However, the main hall was rebuilt in the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]] (1730), and the Daisidō (Great Master Hall) was rebuilt in the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]] (1742). An illustration in the "Shikoku Henrei Meisho Zukai" (published in 1800) shows a form close to the present one.
On [[:en:Mount Kabuto|Koyama (Kagawa)]] (elevation 87.2 m), it is said that during the Muromachi period an outpost of [[:en:Amagiri Castle|Amagiri Castle]] once stood, evidenced by a stone stage on the summit and the presence of a commemorative stone dedicated to Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]] and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Kōmyō]]. In addition, along the mountain path from the temple to the summit, stone Buddhas—dating from the late Edo period and part of the [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]—are arrayed.<ref>According to local signage.</ref>
== Temple Complex == {{File:Kouyamaji kyuusannmonn1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Main Gate}} {{File:Kouyamaji bisyamongutu1.jpg|100px|thumb|Old Bishamonten Cave}} * **Main Gate:** Fell into disrepair in 2008. * **Middle Gate:** Rebuilt in 1769 (the 6th year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]) as the middle gate. * **Main Hall:** Completed in 1735 (the 20th year of the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō era]]). Visitors are required to remove their footwear to view the principal image. The principal image is a seated statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (a monolithic carving from Hiwamatsu with carved eyes); the attendant Buddhas include a standing statue of {{ill|Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|ja|日光月光菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Gekkō Bosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a standing statue of [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]] on the right flank, and a seated statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] on the left. * **Daisidō (Great Master Hall):** Completed in 1742 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Kanpō|Kanpō era]]). A statue of the Great Master in black robes was unveiled in 2014 and 2020. * **Guest Hall:** Features fusuma painted with the Heart Sutra by calligrapher Kohei Okamoto. * **Goma Hall:** Completed in 1782 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]]). Houses a seated statue of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and two child attendants. * **Tsuya Jizō:** Located to the right in front of the Daisidō; according to tradition, carrying its apron is said to bring about the birth of a child. * **Awashima Daimyōjin Shrine (Hokora)** * **Bell Tower:** Completed in 1752 (the 2nd year of the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]]). * **16 Rabbits:** Derived from depictions of rabbits on the accessory of the attendant Buddha [[:en:Candraprabha|Gekkō Bosatsu]] beside the principal image, rabbits are represented on the interior of the Main and Middle Gates, as well as on the roof of the tea house and in front of the chozubasho.<ref>{{Cite news |和書 |title=Welcome to Rabbit Temple – “16 Rabbits” present on the precincts, also used as talismans at Zentsūji/Koyama-ji |newspaper=Shikoku Shimbun |date=2020-04-03 |url=https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |accessdate=2023-07-15 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200405172931/http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/bl/digital_news/article.aspx?id=K2020040300000001300 |archivedate=2020-04-05 }}</ref> * Proceeding through the Main Gate and past the hand-washing basin to the right, turning left to pass through the Middle Gate leads to a Nōkyō (scripture hall) on the right. A short flight of stone steps then leads to the Main Hall; to its right lies the Goma Hall, and a few steps up on the left is the Daisidō. Beyond that is the Bell Tower, and further on is the entrance to the Bishamonten Cave, within which a stone statue of Bishamonten is enshrined. * **Lodging (Shukubō):** None. * **Parking:** A free large parking lot is available in front of the Main Gate. * **Suijin Shrine (Hokora):** Enshrined at the highest point of the precincts. * **Mini Nishi-koku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage:** Markers along the walking path from the precincts to the summit.
== Cultural Properties == ; National Historic Site * The pilgrimage route of the {{ill|Sanuki Pilgrimage|ja|遍路道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Pilgrimage|1=henro-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11640512}} within the precincts of Koyama-ji – This route originally wound eastward around the northern part of Koyama, reached the old Main Gate, then crossed a stone bridge (now removed) over the Hirota River in front of Zentsūji, continuing south. It was recommended in a report on December 20, Reiwa 6.<ref>https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/51893/kouyamazi_shitei.pdf</ref>
== Transportation == ; **Rail** * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company (JR Shikoku)]], [[:en:Dosan Line|Tosan Line]] – [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] (3.6 km) ; **Road** * Public road: {{ill|Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|ja|香川県道48号善通寺詫間線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefectural Road 48: Zentsūji Tarima Line|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 48|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667552}} – Zentsūji Ballpark (0.3 km) * Expressway: [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]], {{ill|Zentsūji Interchange|ja|善通寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsūji Interchange|1=Zentsuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10923476}} (4.5 km)
== Preceding and Succeeding Pilgrimage Sites == ; [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Temples]] : 73: {{ill|Dushakka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dushakka-ji|1=Shusshaka-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395826}} --{{small|(2.2 km)}}-* '''74: Koyama-ji''' --{{small|(1.6 km)}}-- 75: [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsūji]]
